  "findings": [
    "Splice located outside of an enclosure or junction box, exposing live connections to physical damage and creating fire/shock hazard",
    "Unenclosed splice violates NEC 110.14 requirements for electrical connections to be installed in suitable enclosures",
    "In-service unenclosed splice presents ongoing risk of arc fault, short circuit, and personnel contact with energized conductors"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "De-energize the affected circuit immediately",
    "Install a listed junction box or enclosure at the splice location",
    "Make all splices inside the enclosure using approved methods (wire nuts, compression connectors, or other listed means)",
    "Verify proper conductor fill, grounding, and cover installation before re-energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "An in-service splice outside a box is a direct NEC 110.14 violation because electrical connections must be accessible and enclosed to protect against physical damage, fire, and accidental contact. The splice is non-compliant and installed-defective since it was placed in an unapproved location, creating high risk in an operating asset.",
  "references": ["NEC 110.14"]
}
